name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 17.11.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 17.11.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 14, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 208.19 lakh ha (including 64.23 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 37.43 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 188.73 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on November 14, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 208.19 lakh ha (including 64.23 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 37.43 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 188.73 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  14.11.2025,  50.73%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 57% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (84%), Moongbean (98%) & Mothbean (100%);  89.23%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (81.95%),    Soyabean  (99.98%)& Sesamum (81.44%); 80.55% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (84.58%) & Bajra (93.85%) and 38% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  14.11.2025,  50.73%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately, 57% area of Pulses mainly in Uradbean (84%), Moongbean (98%) & Mothbean (100%);  89.23%  area  of  Oilseeds  majorly  in  Groundnut  (81.95%),    Soyabean  (99.98%)& Sesamum (81.44%); 80.55% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (84.58%) & Bajra (93.85%) and 38% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in most of India except in some parts of NE India and J&K. No large change in minimum temperature likely over northwest, central & east India during the week.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in most of India except in some parts of NE India and J&K. No large change in minimum temperature likely over northwest, central & east India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  16  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  30%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  13%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  111%;  (iii)  Central  India:  56%;  and South Peninsula: 8%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  November  16  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  30%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  13%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  111%;  (iii)  Central  India:  56%;  and South Peninsula: 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A low pressure area has formed over Southwest Bay of Bengal off Sri Lanka coast. Under its  influence, heavy to very heavy rainfall Tamil Nadu during 18 th  -22 nd , Kerala & Mahe during 16 th - 20 th ; over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam, Rayalseema on 17 th  & 18 th  and Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 18 th  -21 st  November
texts.text:  A low pressure area has formed over Southwest Bay of Bengal off Sri Lanka coast. Under its  influence, heavy to very heavy rainfall Tamil Nadu during 18 th  -22 nd , Kerala & Mahe during 16 th - 20 th ; over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam, Rayalseema on 17 th  & 18 th  and Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 18 th  -21 st  November

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Light to moderate rainfall/thunderstorm at most/many places with isolated heavy rainfall very  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu  during  18 th   -22 nd ,  Kerala  &  Mahe  during  17 th    -  20 th ;  over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam, Rayalseema on 17 th  & 18 th  and Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 18 th  -21 st  November; with isolated very Heavy rainfall over Tamil Nadu on 17 th  November.
texts.text:  Light to moderate rainfall/thunderstorm at most/many places with isolated heavy rainfall very  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu  during  18 th   -22 nd ,  Kerala  &  Mahe  during  17 th    -  20 th ;  over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam, Rayalseema on 17 th  & 18 th  and Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 18 th  -21 st  November; with isolated very Heavy rainfall over Tamil Nadu on 17 th  November.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall rainfall activity is likely to below normal over all the homogenous regions of India.
texts.text:  Overall rainfall activity is likely to below normal over all the homogenous regions of India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No  significant  change  in  minimum  temperature  likely  over  plains  of  northwest  India, central,  east  and  north  Peninsular  India  during  next  three  days  and  slight  rise  likely thereafter.
texts.text:  No  significant  change  in  minimum  temperature  likely  over  plains  of  northwest  India, central,  east  and  north  Peninsular  India  during  next  three  days  and  slight  rise  likely thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  13  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  163.824  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  89.8%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.26 percent of last  year's storage  and 120.10 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  13  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  163.824  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  89.8%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 106.26 percent of last  year's storage  and 120.10 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Chilli  (Bacterial  leaf  Black  thrips)  in  Uttar  Pradesh.  The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained  below  the  Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Chilli  (Bacterial  leaf  Black  thrips)  in  Uttar  Pradesh.  The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained  below  the  Economic Threshold Level.

texts.text:  During the week (30 Oct - 05 Nov 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 93%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 05 Nov 2025) is higher than LPA by 45%.
